title: Hot rack compute node drain assessment
difficulty_justification: Requires cross-referencing BMS thermal alarm data with SLURM node allocation, then deciding whether draining is warranted.
query_text: Are any currently allocated compute nodes located in racks with active CRITICAL thermal warnings? Should I drain them, and if so, which ones?

eval_criteria.gold_answer: Yes. Racks R-C04 and R-C05 have CRITICAL alarms (>35°C inlet) and contain nodes node201–node208 (8 nodes total). Per the cooling ops runbook, when row inlet exceeds 35°C the sysadmin should contact facilities and consider draining hot-row nodes. Recommendation: drain nodes node201–node208 (scontrol update NodeName=node201,node202,node203,node204,node205,node206,node207,node208 State=DRAIN Reason=cooling_alert), notify facilities team, monitor CRAC-07 status. Jobs will be requeued automatically.

checkpoints.description: Agent cross-references rack alarm with node list.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: final_answer lists node201-node208 or equivalent 8-node set

checkpoints.description: Agent recommends draining the hot nodes.
Agent cites cooling ops runbook for procedure.
